title: ffi: ClientEvent::Disconnected silently dropped; Dart event loop never terminates cleanly
description: In ffi/src/api/client.rs client_connect(), the transport event bridge task matches ClientEvent::Disconnected => None, discarding the event. When the relay disconnects and connect_with_retry gives up after MAX_RETRIES (10) failures, it sends ClientEvent::Disconnected. The bridge task converts this to None and produces no NieEvent. The event_tx channel is then dropped (task exits), so event_rx.recv() returns None. Dart's async* loop exits when client_next_event returns None — this is the expected behavior and documented. BUT: if the relay permanently drops and the task exits without sending FatalError, Dart gets a silent None with no indication of whether the channel closed cleanly or due to error. The FatalError path only fires on panic. Dart cannot distinguish 'graceful shutdown' from 'permanent network failure' — both look like channel closed.
